Petition For Appointment Of C.E. Hader As Successor Trustee Pursant To Settlement Agreement.
Off Calendar Jul-06-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Rapaport for Dana Lurie & David Lurie. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo. Brian Rocca for Robert A. Lurie. David Jolivet for William Fay, Hannah Lurie, Jack Lurie, Robert L. Fay, Olivia R. Fay, Juliette Weis & Milo Black. Edward Koplowitz for Robert L. Lurie, Betsy Weis, Ann Black & Patricia Lurie. Monica Dell'Osso, Guardian ad Litem for the unborn issue of the biological children of Robert Lurie. Lisa Kobialka for (successor trustees) Joseph Toce & Cheryl Harder who are present in court and (petitioners) Robert L. Lurie, Betsy Weiss, Anne Black & Patricia Lurie.

Mini-Minutes part 2 of 2.
***the following is the continuation of mini-minutes for this date.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that WF has been paid millions to administer the trust, that the requested information should have already been provided and that it is their duty to turn this information over. Mr. Rapaport stated that paragraph 6 maintains that the trustees retain trustee-client privilege and that it is best to keep to what the existing order says. Mr. Zabronsky stated that they are not saying that WF does not have a duty, that they should not have re-written what was previously agreed on and that they needed to ask for instructions. The Court asked Mr. Zabronsky if he wanted time for further briefing or another hearing date. Mr. Zabronsky stated that in regards to the first proposed order he requests that they take out paragraph 3, and that in regards to the second proposed order that they quote from the settlement agreement that everyone is to work cooperatively. Mr. Koplowitz stated that it does not say anything about duty of cooperation, asked why they have not responded to the letter, that the trust is being administered out of Minneapolis, that they do not have any contact information, that they are requesting a minimal amount of cooperation and the beneficiaries interest seem to be secondary to WF. Ms. Kobialka stated that they would like a printout of the current statements by tomorrow, followed by 3 years worth of statements and the name of a contact person at WF. Mr. Zabronsky stated that the biological children receive statements every 3 months, that they have sent the last 3 years worth of statements on multiple occasions, that it is not a problem to provide statements but that it is inappropriate to ask for what is not in the petition. Mr. Koplowitz stated that WF should have taken the initiative in providing the information. Mr. Zabronsky stated that even if the proposed orders were signed today it would not be effective for 5 business days and that the proposed successor trustees are requesting documents prior to being appointed. Mr. Koplowitz stated that they will need the documents on whatever day they become appointed and that they do not have confidence in WF providing such. The court asked if there was any possibility that the parties could reach a revised order that would accommodate everyone. The parties provided proposed orders to the court. Mr. Koplowitz advised the court of petitions that would be filed in the next 60 days regarding two smaller trusts that they would like to terminate and distribute and that if they cannot be terminated will need successor trustees. Mr. Zabronsky stated that there is no objection to the termination and that WF is committed to continue as trustee on the small trusts for 60 days only. The petitions for appointment of Joseph P. Toce and C.E. Hader as successor trustees were taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Reporter: Carol Worsdell, CSR #11365, 415-227-4060 (Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Joseph P. Toce, Jr. As Successor Trustee Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-06-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Rapaport for Dana Lurie & David Lurie. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo. Brian Rocca for Robert A. Lurie. David Jolivet for William Fay, Hannah Lurie, Jack Lurie, Robert L. Fay, Olivia R. Fay, Juliette Weis & Milo Black. Edward Koplowitz for Robert L. Lurie, Betsy Weis, Ann Black & Patricia Lurie. Monica Dell'Osso, Guardian ad Litem for the unborn issue of the biological children of Robert Lurie. Lisa Kobialka for (successor trustees) Joseph Toce & Cheryl Harder who are present in court and (petitioners) Robert L. Lurie, Betsy Weiss, Anne Black & Patricia Lurie.

Mini-minutes part 1 of 2

Ms. Kobialka reported that the proof of service has been filed and served on everyone, that everyone is here and provided a copy to the court. Mr. Zabronsky provided oral argument regarding the objection, stated that the proposed trustee is the biological children's litigation attorney, that they have sent a letter to Wells Fargo (WF) requesting documents with an unreasonable deadline and that it is contrary to the settlement agreement that states everyone will work together. Mr. Koplowitz stated that it deals with one paragraph of the proposed order, that WF has made many mistakes, that WF has cost the trust millions in fees, that this issue is in regards to materials that should have already been ready to go, that the letter was sent so that there would be no ambiguity, that the outgoing trustee should want protection so that they do not get blamed later, that the successor trustees are going to be responsible for $90,000,000.00 in assets, that the beneficiaries have a right to a seamless transition and read to the court the language in the letter being objected to. Ms. Kobialka stated that they communicated with counsel as they have no contact at WF, that the successor trustees need to be aware of all of the assets and that they have a lot of work to do in a short period of time. Mr. Rocca stated that they had addressed this in the settlement agreement, that they were to work promptly for the transition to take place and requested that the order parrot the language of the settlement agreement. Mr. Zabronsky stated that they are not saying that WF would not perform their duties if not capsulated in the order, that the issues should have been brought forth in the petition, that on Wednesday he received the proposed order which should have been delivered to the court 10 days prior to the hearing, that in regards to the letter they take exception to the long list of bullet points and the due dates, that WF understands its duties and that the order conflicts with the settlement agreement.

***Mini-minutes continued on next entry for this date.***

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Reporter: Carol Worsdell, CSR #11365, 415-227-4060 (Reported)

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And For Modification Of The Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust Dated August 25, 1992
Off Calendar Jun-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-89-201334 Estate of Louis R. Lurie & PTR-13-296844 The Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust (RALFIT).

Appearances: Daniel Rapaport & Virginia Palmer for Dana Lurie who is present. Ernest Der for (successor trustee) Debra Dolch who is present. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo. Ali Gramaglia for Robert A. Lurie who is present. David Jolivet for William Fay, Hannah Lurie, Jack Lurie, Robert L. Fay, Olivia R. Fay, Juliette Weis & Milo Black. Edward Koplowitz & Andrew Timkin for Robert Lurie, Betsy Weis, Ann Black & Patricia Lurie. Lawrence Siracusa, Guardian ad Litem for the unborn children of the adopted children. Monica Dell'Osso (via court call) Guardian ad Litem for the unborn issue of the biological children of Robert Lurie. Brian Rocca (via court call) for Robert A. Lurie.

The court stated that we are here for the petition for approval of settlement and modification of the trust, that it is pleased to see all of the parties here and able to reach a settlement, that it has reviewed the paperwork, that it appears appropriate but there are some issues. The court addressed the proposed order indicating that separate orders are needed for the two cases, requested that the signature page be modified to include some text, that some of the findings are not appropriate to be in the order and requested that the revised order be emailed to her in the complex litigation department. Mr. Koplowitz stated that the settlement agreement and the proposed order have been revised extensively, that administration of the trust will go on indefinitely and that historical recitation makes sense to that years from now it will be helpful. The court agreed that it could be left in, stated that there was no bond requirement for the successor trustee, and that per page 6 the biological children could appoint a successor trustee. Mr. Koplowitz stated that it was on calendar for 7/1/15. The court stated that issue regarding bond may come up on 7/1/15, that language in the order regarding jurisdiction was not in the settlement agreement and that the clients must be here to make the change. Mr. Rapaport stated that it was envisioned after the approval and responded to the question regarding the ages of Dana (47) and David (45). The court asked if there was a spendthrift provision, stated that the subtrust is to be terminated at age 50, confirmed that the implications have been discussed with the clients and asked who will be paying the Guardian ad Litems. Mr. Siracusa stated that he will be filing for fees and discharge and understood his fees would be paid out of RALFIT. Ms. Dell'Osso stated that she is not sure who the source of payment of her fees is. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he expected they will come from the remaining 2 trusts. Mr. Rocca stated that they need to check whether it is appropriate to pay her fees from RALFIT or the remaining trusts. Mr. Koplowitz expressed concern regarding the petitions on calendar for 7/1/15, that people will be flying in from New York, the need to get the draft out and the order signed quickly and that interlineations on the orders sometimes create issues. The court addressed the issue of bond regarding the 7/1/15 hearing and that per local rule it is required on successor trustees unless waived. Mr. Der stated that an bond in the amount of $11,000,000.00 exists on the RALFIT and can remain in place. The court stated that once the subtrusts are created Dana & David can waive bond and reminded counsel to update appearances for today on the order, add text to the signature page and to email an order for each case to her. The Court granted the petition for approval of settlement agreement and modification of the Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust (both cases)(subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Feb-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for and with Patricia Lurie, and for Robert L. Lurie, Babette Weis and Anne Black. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank. Daniel Rapaport for and with Dana Lurie and for David Lurie. Bette Epstein for James Hunt. Brian Rocca for Robert A. Lurie. David Baer for William Faye, Olivia Faye and Robert Faye (Great-grandchildren of the Decedent).

Discussion was held with regard to the tentative ruling, interpretation/definition of the words "issue" and "children", construction of the will, whether the Parties would consider mediation, Mr. Zabronsky amending his petition for instructions to take a position, what the scope of discovery should be, what the Trustor's intent was, to what extent the Trustees are carrying out the Trustor's intent, case management criteria, whether the $6 million dollar case should/should not be bifurcated, whether or not all matters should be handled concurrently, how fees for litigation are to be paid, the order of adoption of Dana and David, appointment of a Discovery Referee, the Parties stipulating to the length of time/additional time for depositions, whether the adopted children are included in the gift, etc. The Motion for Protective Order was taken under submission. Case management was discussed. The will court issue two separate orders. Mr. Baer's current ex parte petitions for appointment of Guardian Ad Litem are withdrawn and new ones will be filed. The Parties stipulate to Mr. Zabronsky amending the petition for instructions (amended petition to be filed by March 14, 2014). The Trustees responses to the cross petitions are to be filed by March 21, 2014. All matters currently set for March 26, 2014 are continued to April 2, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the pleadings. The Court invited Counsel to file case management conference statements/briefs prior to April 2, 2014. The Court encouraged the Parties to discuss appointment of a Probate Referee, the limits on depositions and to consider mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Carol Worsdell, CSR# 11365, (415) 227-4060. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen.
